title: lathyrus vernus subsp. gracilis
intro: Lathyrus vernus subsp. gracilis, also known as the spring pea or woodland pea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that provide various health benefits, such as protecting against oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Lathyrus vernus subsp. gracilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
